def restrict_scalars.alg_equiv (R : Type u_1) (S : Type u_2) (A : Type u_4) [comm_semiring S] [semiring A] [algebra S A] :
restrict_scalars R S A ≃ₐ[S] A

Tautological S-algebra isomorphism restrict_scalars R S A ≃ₐ[S] A.

Equations
  • restrict_scalars.alg_equiv R S A = alg_equiv.refl
source
@[instance]
def restrict_scalars.algebra (R : Type u_1) (S : Type u_2) (A : Type u_4) [comm_semiring S] [semiring A] [algebra S A] [comm_semiring R] [algebra R S] :
algebra R (restrict_scalars R S A)

R ⟶ S induces S-Alg ⥤ R-Alg

Equations
  • restrict_scalars.algebra R S A = {to_has_scalar := {smul := has_scalar.smul smul_with_zero.to_has_scalar}, to_ring_hom := {to_fun := ((algebra_map S A).comp (algebra_map R S)).to_fun, map_one' := _, map_mul' := _, map_zero' := _, map_add' := _}, commutes' := _, smul_def' := _}

TODO: The following lemmas no longer involve algebra at all, and could be moved closer to algebra/module/submodule.lean. Currently this is tricky because ker, range, ⊤, and ⊥ are all defined in linear_algebra/basic.lean.

source
def submodule.restrict_scalars (R : Type u_1) {S : Type u_2} {M : Type u_3} [semiring R] [semiring S] [has_scalar R S] [add_comm_monoid M] [module R M] [module S M] [is_scalar_tower R S M] (V : submodule S M) :
submodule R M

V.restrict_scalars R is the R-submodule of the R-module given by restriction of scalars, corresponding to V, an S-submodule of the original S-module.

Equations
  • submodule.restrict_scalars R V = {carrier := V.carrier, zero_mem' := _, add_mem' := _, smul_mem' := _}
source
@[simp]
theorem submodule.restrict_scalars_carrier (R : Type u_1) {S : Type u_2} {M : Type u_3} [semiring R] [semiring S] [has_scalar R S] [add_comm_monoid M] [module R M] [module S M] [is_scalar_tower R S M] (V : submodule S M) :
(submodule.restrict_scalars R V).carrier = V.carrier
source
@[simp]
theorem submodule.restrict_scalars_mem (R : Type u_1) {S : Type u_2} {M : Type u_3} [semiring R] [semiring S] [has_scalar R S] [add_comm_monoid M] [module R M] [module S M] [is_scalar_tower R S M] (V : submodule S M) (m : M) :
m ∈ submodule.restrict_scalars R V ↔ m ∈ V
source
theorem submodule.restrict_scalars_injective (R : Type u_1) (S : Type u_2) (M : Type u_3) [semiring R] [semiring S] [has_scalar R S] [add_comm_monoid M] [module R M] [module S M] [is_scalar_tower R S M] :
function.injective (submodule.restrict_scalars R)
source
@[simp]
theorem submodule.restrict_scalars_inj (R : Type u_1) (S : Type u_2) (M : Type u_3) [semiring R] [semiring S] [has_scalar R S] [add_comm_monoid M] [module R M] [module S M] [is_scalar_tower R S M] {V₁ V₂ : submodule S M} :
submodule.restrict_scalars R V₁ = submodule.restrict_scalars R V₂ ↔ V₁ = V₂
source
@[simp]
theorem submodule.restrict_scalars_bot (R : Type u_1) (S : Type u_2) (M : Type u_3) [semiring R] [semiring S] [has_scalar R S] [add_comm_monoid M] [module R M] [module S M] [is_scalar_tower R S M] :
submodule.restrict_scalars R ⊥ = ⊥
source
@[simp]
theorem submodule.restrict_scalars_top (R : Type u_1) (S : Type u_2) (M : Type u_3) [semiring R] [semiring S] [has_scalar R S] [add_comm_monoid M] [module R M] [module S M] [is_scalar_tower R S M] :
submodule.restrict_scalars R ⊤ = ⊤
source
theorem submodule.span_le_restrict_scalars (R : Type u_1) (S : Type u_2) (M : Type u_3) [semiring R] [semiring S] [has_scalar R S] [add_comm_monoid M] [module R M] [module S M] [is_scalar_tower R S M] (X : set M) :
submodule.span R X ≤ submodule.restrict_scalars R (submodule.span S X)

If S is an R-algebra, then the R-module generated by a set X is included in the S-module generated by X.

source
@[simp]
theorem linear_map.ker_restrict_scalars (R : Type u_1) {S : Type u_2} {M : Type u_3} {N : Type u_4} [semiring R] [semiring S] [has_scalar R S] [add_comm_monoid M] [module R M] [module S M] [is_scalar_tower R S M] [add_comm_monoid N] [module R N] [module S N] [is_scalar_tower R S N] (f : M →ₗ[S] N) :
(linear_map.restrict_scalars R f).ker = submodule.restrict_scalars R f.ker
source
theorem submodule.span_eq_restrict_scalars (R : Type u_1) (A : Type u_2) (M : Type u_3) [comm_semiring R] [semiring A] [algebra R A] [add_comm_monoid M] [module R M] [module A M] [is_scalar_tower R A M] (X : set M) (hsur : function.surjective ⇑(algebra_map R A)) :
submodule.span R X = submodule.restrict_scalars R (submodule.span A X)

If A is an R-algebra such that the induced morhpsim R →+* A is surjective, then the R-module generated by a set X equals the A-module generated by X.

source
def alg_hom.comp_left {R : Type u} {A : Type v} {B : Type w} [comm_semiring R] [semiring A] [semiring B] [algebra R A] [algebra R B] (f : A →ₐ[R] B) (I : Type u_1) :
(I → A) →ₐ[R] I → B

R-algebra homomorphism between the function spaces I → A and I → B, induced by an R-algebra homomorphism f between A and B.

Equations
  • f.comp_left I = {to_fun := λ (h : I → A), ⇑f ∘ h, map_one' := _, map_mul' := _, map_zero' := _, map_add' := _, commutes' := _}
source
@[simp]
theorem alg_hom.comp_left_apply {R : Type u} {A : Type v} {B : Type w} [comm_semiring R] [semiring A] [semiring B] [algebra R A] [algebra R B] (f : A →ₐ[R] B) (I : Type u_1) (h : I → A) (ᾰ : I) :
⇑(f.comp_left I) h ᾰ = (⇑f ∘ h) ᾰ
